skill.name: helpdesk-automation
skill.description: Automate HelpDesk tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): list tickets, manage views, use canned responses, and configure custom fields. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate HelpDesk ticketing operations via Rube MCP - list tickets, manage views, use canned responses, inspect custom fields.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides documentation for interacting with HelpDesk ticketing system via Rube MCP (Composio). All static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting for tool/parameter names, the URL is a legitimate public service endpoint, and sort order abbreviations were misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms. No actual security risks detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Backtick Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged Ruby/shell backtick execution at multiple lines. These are markdown code formatting for tool names (RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS, HELPDESK_LIST_TICKETS) and parameter names (silo, sortBy, order). No actual command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown are used exclusively for code formatting of tool and parameter names. No shell execution or dynamic command building present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'asc' and 'desc' as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are sort order abbreviations (ascending/descending) used for the sortBy parameter.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The words 'asc' and 'desc' appear in context of sort order ('asc' or 'desc'), not cryptography. This is a misidentification by the static analyzer.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged a hardcoded URL at line 22. This is the legitimate public endpoint for Rube MCP service (https://rube.app/mcp), not a malicious URL.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL https://rube.app/mcp is the documented public endpoint for the Rube MCP service, required for the skill to function. This is a legitimate service URL, not an indicator of malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: This skill automates HelpDesk ticketing operations via Rube MCP, enabling users to list tickets, manage views, access canned responses, and inspect custom fields through Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: List and browse support tickets with sorting and pagination
View saved agent views for organizing tickets
Access canned response templates for common replies
Inspect custom field definitions for account-specific data
limitations: Read-only operations only - cannot create, update, or reply to tickets
Requires active Rube MCP connection with HelpDesk toolkit
Ticket creation and update operations may require additional tools
Rate limited by HelpDesk API per-account limits

use_cases.title: Support Agent Ticket Review
use_cases.description: Support agents can quickly browse and paginate through tickets to review pending work across different folders (active, archived, trashed, spam).

use_cases.title: Knowledge Base Access
use_cases.description: Agents can access canned responses to quickly find template replies for common customer questions without typing from scratch.

use_cases.title: Custom Field Discovery
use_cases.description: Administrators or developers can inspect custom field definitions to understand what extra data fields are available in the ticketing system.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Tickets
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the helpdesk-automation skill to list the 10 most recent support tickets sorted by creation date.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to see the latest incoming support requests

prompt_templates.title: Browse Ticket Archive
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the helpdesk-automation skill to list archived tickets from the last week, sorted by update time.

prompt_templates.title: Find Canned Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the helpdesk-automation skill to list all available canned responses for quick reply templates.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing to respond to common customer issues

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Custom Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the helpdesk-automation skill to list all custom field definitions available in the HelpDesk system.
prompt_templates.scenario: When integrating with the ticketing system or understanding data structure

output_examples.input: List 5 recent tickets sorted by creation date
output_examples.output: Returns a list of 5 tickets with IDs, subjects, status, created timestamps, and pagination cursors for retrieving more results.
Returns an array of canned response templates with IDs, titles, and content that can be used as reply templates.
Returns custom field definitions including field names, types, and validation rules for the HelpDesk account.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before starting workflows
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any HelpDesk operations
Implement cursor-based pagination correctly by using both timestamp and ID values together
anti_patterns: Do not assume ticket creation or update tools are available - check RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS for newly added tools
Do not mix forward and backward pagination cursors in the same request
Do not ignore rate limit responses - implement backoff to avoid temporary blocks

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server by Composio that provides access to various toolkits including HelpDesk. Add https://rube.app/mcp as your MCP server to use this skill.

faq.question: Can I create or update tickets with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides read-only operations for listing tickets, views, canned responses, and custom fields. Check RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS for any newly available write operations.

faq.question: How does pagination work?
faq.answer: The HelpDesk API uses cursor-based pagination. You must use both the timestamp (next.value/prev.value) and ID (next.ID/prev.ID) from the previous response to navigate between pages.

faq.question: What is a silo in ticket listing?
faq.answer: Silo refers to the ticket folder. Use 'tickets' for active tickets, 'archive' for archived tickets, 'trash' for deleted tickets, or 'spam' for spam folder.

faq.question: Do I need API keys for Rube MCP?
faq.answer: No API keys are required. Just add the Rube MCP endpoint (https://rube.app/mcp) to your client configuration and authenticate through the HelpDesk connection flow.

faq.question: What are custom fields?
faq.answer: Custom fields are organization-specific data fields that extend the default ticket schema. Administrators configure these in the HelpDesk admin panel to capture additional information.
